texto: My favorite, how did you put it now? Landscapes, animals, plants? Favorite what? Books, music, architecture, painting? I don't have any favorite animals, no favorite mosquitoes, favorite beetles, favorite worms, even with the best will in the world I cannot tell you which birds or fish or predators I prefer, it would also be difficult for me to have to choose much more generally.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ingeborg Bachmann que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “My favorite, how did you put it now? Landscapes, animals, plants? Favorite what? Books, music, ar…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, animals, favorite-stuff, com foco em favorite-stuff e favourite.

texto: A single tear forms, just in the corner of one eye, but it doesn't roll down my cheek; it merely crystallizes in the cold air, it grows and grows into a second giant globe that doesn't want to orbit with the world—it breaks off from the planet and plunges into infinity.

texto: But Beatrix knew very well that there were no jobs, not even the most pitiful office routine - she wasn't even qualified for that - and that no one would allow her to sleep until late in the afternoon because these ill-advised people all around her let themselves be squeezed into schedules; that she would never work, least of all learn a trade, because she had no ambition whatsoever to earn a single shilling, become self-supporting and spend eight hours a day with people who smelled bad.

texto: You wouldn't believe it, but apart from a few drunks, a few sex murderers and other men who get into the papers where they are designated as criminals of passion, no normal man with normal drives has the obvious idea that a normal woman would like to be quite normally raped. Part of it is that men aren't normal, but people are incapable of even imagining all the ramifications of the male disease, so accustomed have they become to men's mistakes in judgment and their phenomenal lack of instinct.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ingeborg Bachmann: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. texto: The children are in love but do not know with what. They talk in gibberish, muse themselves into an indefinable pallor, and when they are completely at a loss they invent a language that maddens them. My fish. My hook. My fox. My snare. My fire. You my water. You my current. My earth. You my if. And you my but. Either. Or. My everything...my everything...They push one another, go for each other with their fists and scuffle over a counter-word that doesn't exist.
explicacao: Citação de Ingeborg Bachmann sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “The children are in love but do not know with what. They talk in gibberish, muse themselves into …” articula reflexao, language, words em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e words.

texto: There are people who think that Malina and I are married. We never considered that we might be married, that such a possibility could exist, nor even the idea that other people might think that we were married. For the longest time it never crossed our minds that, like other people, we appear as man and wife wherever we go. This was a complete surprise for us, but we had no idea what to make of it. We laughed a lot.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ingeborg Bachmann: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “There are people who think that Malina and I are married. texto: As it was all was lost. He was alive, yes, he was alive, he felt this for the first time. But he knew now that he was living in a prison, that he had to make the best of it in there and would soon rage and would have to speak this thieves' cant, the only language at his disposal, in order not to be so abandoned.
